questions.text: A group of cells that are similar in structure and/or work together to achieve a particular function is called:
questions.explanation: Tissues represent a level of organization in multicellular organisms.

questions.text: Plant tissues that have the ability to divide throughout life are:
questions.explanation: Meristematic tissues are growth tissues located at specific regions in plants.

questions.text: Which meristem is responsible for the increase in length of stems and roots?
questions.explanation: Apical meristem is present at the growing tips of stems and roots.

questions.text: Increase in the girth (width) of the stem or root is due to:
questions.explanation: Lateral meristem (cambium) facilitates the lateral growth.

questions.text: The process of taking up a permanent shape, size, and function is called:
questions.explanation: Meristematic cells differentiate to form various types of permanent tissues.

questions.text: Which tissue provides support and also stores food in plants?
questions.explanation: Parenchyma is the most common simple permanent tissue with relatively unspecialized cells.

questions.text: Parenchyma that contains chlorophyll and performs photosynthesis is called:
questions.explanation: Chlorenchyma is found in green leaves and stems.

questions.text: In aquatic plants, large air cavities in parenchyma to give buoyancy are called:
questions.explanation: Aerenchyma helps aquatic plants float.

questions.text: The tissue that allows easy bending in various parts of a plant without breaking is:
questions.explanation: Collenchyma provides flexibility and mechanical support.

questions.text: The tissue that makes the plant hard and stiff (e.g., husk of coconut) is:
questions.explanation: Sclerenchyma consists of long, narrow, dead cells with thickened walls due to lignin.

questions.text: The chemical substance that acts as cement and hardens cell walls in sclerenchyma is:
questions.explanation: Lignin makes the cell walls thick and strong.

questions.text: The outermost layer of cells in plants is called:
questions.explanation: Epidermis is usually one-cell thick and protects the plant.

questions.text: Small pores on the surface of the leaf are called:
questions.explanation: Stomata are necessary for gas exchange and transpiration.

questions.text: The kidney-shaped cells that enclose stomata are:
questions.explanation: Guard cells regulate the opening and closing of stomata.

questions.text: The chemical present in the walls of cork cells that makes them impervious to gases and water is:
questions.explanation: Suberin provides protective properties to the bark/cork of trees.

questions.text: Which of the following are complex permanent tissues?
questions.explanation: Complex tissues are made of more than one type of cells that work together as a unit.

questions.text: Which component of Xylem is living?
questions.explanation: Most xylem cells are dead at maturity, except for xylem parenchyma.

questions.text: Which component of Phloem is non-living?
questions.explanation: Phloem fibres (bast fibres) provide mechanical strength and are dead.

questions.text: Xylem helps in the conduction of:
questions.explanation: Xylem transports water unidirectionally from roots to leaves.

questions.text: Phloem helps in the conduction of:
questions.explanation: Phloem transports food (sucrose) in both directions (translocation).

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a type of animal tissue?
questions.explanation: Meristematic tissue is only found in plants.

questions.text: The covering or protective tissues in the animal body are:
questions.explanation: Epithelium covers most organs and cavities and forms a barrier.

questions.text: The epithelium found in the lining of blood vessels or lung alveoli (where transport occurs) is:
questions.explanation: Simple squamous is thin and flat, allowing easy diffusion.

questions.text: Our skin is made of:
questions.explanation: Multiple layers prevent wear and tear.

questions.text: Ciliated columnar epithelium is found in the:
questions.explanation: Cilia help push mucus forward to clear the tract.

questions.text: Connective tissue that has a liquid matrix called plasma is:
questions.explanation: Blood transports gases, food, hormones, and waste.

questions.text: Ligaments connect:
questions.explanation: Ligaments are very elastic and have great strength.

questions.text: Tendons connect:
questions.explanation: Tendons are fibrous tissues with great strength but limited flexibility.

questions.text: Cartilage is found in:
questions.explanation: Cartilage smoothens bone surfaces at joints and provides structure to ears/nose.

questions.text: The tissue that stores fat in our body is:
questions.explanation: Adipose tissue is found below skin and between internal organs; it acts as an insulator.

questions.text: Muscles that we can move by conscious will are:
questions.explanation: Skeletal muscles are attached to bones and help in body movement.

questions.text: Involuntary muscles found in the alimentary canal are:
questions.explanation: Smooth muscles control automatic movements like peristalsis.

questions.text: Which muscles show rhythmic contraction and relaxation throughout life?
questions.explanation: Cardiac muscles are only found in the heart.

questions.text: The cells of nervous tissue are called:
questions.explanation: Neurons are the functional units of the nervous system.

questions.text: The long part of a neuron is called:
questions.explanation: The axon carries impulses away from the cell body.

questions.text: Areolar connective tissue is found:
questions.explanation: It fills the space inside organs and supports internal organs.

questions.text: Bone cells are embedded in a hard matrix composed of:
questions.explanation: These minerals provide rigidity and strength to bones.

questions.text: Sieve tubes and companion cells are components of:
questions.explanation: They work together to transport organic food.

questions.text: Striated muscles are also called skeletal muscles because:
options: They are mostly attached to bones
questions.explanation: They provide the power for skeletal movement.

questions.text: Nodes of Ranvier are found in:
questions.explanation: They are gaps in the myelin sheath of an axon.
